22.reason: [Error: The semicolon after the while loop ends the loop body, so the following block only executes once after the loop finishes, processing only the last line of the file. Remove the semicolon to process each line.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: Semicolon after while loop causes the loop body to be empty, so the following block (lines 20-44) executes only once after the loop ends, processing only the last line read. The semicolon should be removed.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  The semicolon terminates the loop, so the following block executes only once after the loop ends, processing only the last line (or garbage if file is empty). This causes the program to miss most words.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The semicolon at the end of the while statement creates an empty loop body, causing the program to consume the entire file without executing the processing block. reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The semicolon immediately terminates the while loop, causing the loop body to be skipped. The subsequent block executes only once after EOF, failing to process the file correctly.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The semicolon here terminates the while loop immediately, making the following block execute only once after the loop ends, not per line. This causes the program to process only the last line (or uninitialized data if file is empty).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The semicolon here terminates the while loop immediately, causing the following block to execute only once after the loop ends, not per line. This results in processing only uninitialized or leftover data in `txt`.]

29.reason: [Error: Setting w[j+1] to '\0' here is incorrect because j is the current index; it should be set after incrementing j. This causes the string to be null-terminated one position too early, potentially corrupting the word. The null terminator should be placed at w[j+1] after j is incremented, or better, set w[j] = '\0' after the loop.]
